title: Trainforge Chunk v4
description: Schematizes the per-chunk record emitted by Trainforge's process_course.py pipeline (chunks.jsonl and chunks.json). Each chunk is a retrieval-layer node in the course knowledge graph, carrying text + HTML + source provenance + Bloom's level + content-type metadata. Per REC-CTR-01 this is the first time chunk_v4 is schematized; validation is opt-in via TRAINFORGE_VALIDATE_CHUNKS=true to preserve backward-compat during rollout.
$comment: Shape lifted from Trainforge/process_course.py::_create_chunk at L1038-1240 and _write_chunks at L1667-1682. Verified live against 131 chunks in LibV2/courses/best-practices-in-digital-web-design-for-accessibi/corpus/chunks.jsonl (100% conformant). Root additionalProperties=true preserves Worker M1's _metadata_trace diagnostic (L1219-1228) and leaves room for forward-compat additions. source.additionalProperties=false keeps the structural core strict. Taxonomy $refs target Worker F's schemas (merged in PR #20).

id.description: Globally unique chunk identifier. Default form: '<course_slug>_chunk_<5-digit position>'. When TRAINFORGE_CONTENT_HASH_IDS=true (Worker N, REC-ID-01), form is '<course_slug>_chunk_<16-hex-char sha256 of text|source_locator|schema_version>'; IDs are then stable across re-chunks. Example: wcag_201_chunk_00001 (legacy) or wcag_201_chunk_a3f2b9c8d1e4f567 (content-hash).

schema_version.description: Chunk schema version literal. Downstream readers gate on this to dispatch parser versions without re-reading manifest.json.

chunk_type.description: Chunk content classification: assessment_item, overview, summary, exercise, explanation, or example. Lifted from Worker F's content_type taxonomy.

text.description: Plain-text extraction used for retrieval. Post-canonicalize_sc_references (WCAG SC references collapsed to canonical form).

html.description: Original HTML fragment the chunk was derived from. Preserved for downstream rendering.

follows_chunk.description: ID of the immediately preceding chunk in the same module, or null for the first chunk.

concept_tags.description: Normalized concept slugs extracted from text + key terms (see process_course.py::_extract_concept_tags).

learning_outcome_refs.description: IDs of learning outcomes this chunk supports. minItems:0 permits chunks without LO refs (common in non-Courseforge IMSCC imports); REC-CTR-01's 'at least 1' aspiration deferred to an LO-backfill wave.

bloom_level.description: Always populated. Resolution cascade (process_course.py:1099-1140): section JSON-LD > page JSON-LD > parsed LO > text verb heuristic > 'understand' default.

bloom_level_source.description: Provenance tag for bloom_level. Only emitted when source is 'verbs' or 'default' (low-confidence paths), per process_course.py:1145.

content_type_label.description: Section/content classification label from JSON-LD or data-cf-content-type. Freeform today; future enum reconciliation is Wave 2 scope.

misconceptions.description: Per-chunk misconceptions normalized from page-level JSON-LD. Union permits dict (Courseforge-emitted) or plain string (non-Courseforge IMSCC passthrough); see process_course.py:1177-1184.

summary.description: Deterministic extractive summary from Trainforge/generators/summary_factory.py for dense-retrieval recall augmentation.

retrieval_text.description: summary + ' ' + key_terms_joined. Emitted only when key_terms present (benchmarked in Trainforge/rag/retrieval_benchmark.py).

_metadata_trace.description: Worker M1 (VERSIONING.md §4.4a) diagnostic — records the source path for each enrichment field. Removed by a later fix PR; schema permits its presence to keep diagnostic runs valid.

run_id.description: Pipeline run that produced this chunk (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Matches decision_event.schema.json run_id format — sourced from the active DecisionCapture instance's run_id. Always populated on new runs; legacy chunks without this field still validate.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the chunk was emitted (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Produced by datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). Always populated on new runs; legacy chunks without this field still validate.

Source.description: Chunk provenance block. Per REC-CTR-01 course_id/module_id/lesson_id are the structural core and strictly required; additionalProperties=false prevents silent drift. Audit-trail fields (html_xpath, char_span, item_path) support the Section 508 / ADA Title II round-trip contract documented at docs/compliance/audit-trail.md. Wave 10 adds optional source_references[] threading DART/Courseforge provenance end-to-end; see schemas/knowledge/source_reference.schema.json for the canonical shape.

module_title.description: Human-readable module title. Optional for non-Courseforge IMSCC imports that lack module naming.

section_heading.description: Heading of the section this chunk came from. May carry '(part N)' suffix for multi-part text blocks.

html_xpath.description: XPath into the source IMSCC HTML element (audit-trail; enables element_text = resolve_xpath(raw_html, html_xpath)).

char_span.description: [start, end] character offset range within the xpath-resolved element.

item_path.description: IMSCC-relative path to the source HTML file, for auditor navigation without imsmanifest.xml traversal.

source_references.description: Wave 10: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references threaded from Courseforge JSON-LD sourceReferences[] and data-cf-source-ids HTML attributes. Always an array because _merge_small_sections may collapse 2+ sections into one chunk, each potentially carrying its own refs. Absence = 'unknown' (pre-Wave-9 corpora) — never an error. Populated by process_course.py::_create_chunk; unioned with role-precedence (primary > contributing > corroborating) when sections merge.
